channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Thick As Thieves priced at $4.99, launch to serve as ‘introductory campaign’
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Developer plans to expand the setting and stories across future content.

Stealth action heist game Thick As Thieves will be priced at $4.99 / €4.99 / £4.99 when it launches for PC via Steam on May 20, and the initial release will serve as an introductory campaign for future content, Megabit Publishing and developer OtherSide Entertainment announced.

“Kilcairn is an early 1900s alternative history city setting we’ve always wanted to explore beyond a single story,” said OtherSide Entertainment CEO Paul Neurath in a press release. “Releasing this introductory campaign gets players into the world of Thick As Thieves and gives them a meaty taste of a world worth returning to.”

The introductory campaign, which will span “at least four hours,” will feature 16 missions set across two dynamic replayable maps, along with six pieces of gear to unlock.

According to Megabit Publishing, the intention going forward is to expand the setting and stories of Thick As Thieves across future content, and the decision to launch as an introductory campaign “reflects the team’s desire to bring players into the world sooner, and to give the team the flexibility to develop additional content informed by how players engage with the game.”

Thick As Thieves is due out for PC via Steam on May 20, followed by PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series at a later date.

Watch a new developer walkthrough below.

Developer Walkthrough: Welcome to Kilcairn



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/thick-as-thieves-priced-at-4-99-launch-to-serve-as-introductory-campaign
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Phonopolis launches May 20
item.content:encoded:
• PC

3D puzzle adventure game by Amanita Design.

Phonopolis, the 3D puzzle adventure game by Amanita Design first announced in May 2022, will launch for PC via Steam, Epic Games Store, and GOG on May 20, the developer announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Amanita Design:

In Phonopolis, players take on the role of Felix, a regular garbage collector who faces the imminent threat of falling under the control of the city’s authoritarian Leader. Accidentally becoming the only person who consciously recognizes the threat, Felix must stop the authoritarian Leader from playing the “Absolute Tone” and turning all citizens into mindless servants forever.

Alongside the game’s release, Amanita Design is preparing to launch a documentary film capturing the game’s unique and lengthy development process. Directed by Ales Brunclik, Constructing Phonopolis will be available to watch in full on YouTube shortly before the game launches.

Unlike Amanita’s previous titles, which relied on non-verbal communication, Phonopolis features a fully voiced narration in English by Joe Acheson, known for his music project Hidden Orchestra. To make sure players worldwide can enjoy Felix’s story, the game features subtitles localized into 17 additional languages as listed on the game’s Steam page. The original score was composed and produced by Tomas Dvorak aka Floex, with sound effects provided by Matous Godik.

In February 2026, Amanita Design released a public demo of Phonopolis on Steam which has received over 600 user reviews, 99 percent of them being positive, securing the mythical “Overwhelmingly Positive” rating.

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/phonopolis-launches-may-20
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
